exemplar.title: Investigating the effects of the specific heat capacity of a liquid on its change in  temperature

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating the effects of the specific heat capacity of a liquid on its change in  temperature

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables and graphs are presented clearly with units and a fitted line
Uncertainties for temperature and specific heat are quoted, and formula Q=mcÎT is correctly introduced
Communication of raw and processed data is generally precise
weaknesses: Major conceptual and numerical errors in processing (unit mix-ups, doubled specific heat values)
Incomplete uncertainty propagation (mass and power uncertainties ignored, missing error bars)
Mis-labelled units and inconsistent sample calculations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data is clear and precise in most respects (band 3â4), and there is some evidence of uncertainties (band 3â4), but major processing inaccuracies place this work at the top of the 3â4 band. A score of 4 best reflects these strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Step-by-step procedures with apparatus list and diagrams enable clear reproducibility
Comprehensive control of variables (mass, time, power, insulation) with three repeats
Detailed assumptions table and rationale for selecting five liquids with distinct properties
weaknesses: Research question context remains broad and lacks anchoring in a specific real-world scenario
Minor missing details (exact kettle model, calibration data) could impede precision
Brief explanations of why each assumption holds could be expanded
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student provides an excellent methodological description and clear reproducibility (band 5â6), the context remains at a broad level (band 3â4). On balance, a score of 5 reflects strong performance with only minor omissions.

strengths: Conclusion restates the observed inverse trend and cites a high correlation from the graph
Connects findings to the First Law of Thermodynamics and mentions a percentage error
Attempts to compare to literature values
weaknesses: No quantitative comparison to actual literature values or calculation shown for percentage error
Does not address how analysis inaccuracies affect the validity of the model fit
Superficial discussion of scientific context without numerical benchmarks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is consistent with the studentâs analysis and theory (band 3â4) but lacks full justification and detailed comparison to accepted values (band 1â2). An overall score of 3 reflects a conclusion that is coherent but not fully supported.

strengths: Identifies specific methodological weaknesses (heat loss, impurities, stirring inconsistencies, timing errors) and describes their qualitative impact
Proposes realistic and relevant improvements (insulation, pure substances, automated timing, constant-power heater) linked to identified weaknesses
Considers thoughtful future extensions with new liquids and methods
weaknesses: Does not quantify or rank the relative impact of each limitation
Explanations of expected accuracy gains from suggested improvements are brief
Lacks a deeper analysis of which improvement would yield the greatest benefit
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes multiple specific weaknesses and offers realistic, relevant improvements with rationale (solidly in the 3â4 band) and edges toward explanation of impact. Assigning 5 acknowledges the strong, well-linked improvements despite some lack of quantification.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is clearly stated and linked to the general field of energetics and sustainable design, but the context remains broad (generic references to climate change and engineering applications).  The student does not anchor the question in a very specific, well-defined scientific or real-life situation.  Hence the work satisfies the descriptor for âoutlined within a broad context,â warranting the middle of the 3-4 band.

Instrument uncertainties are identified, and combined uncertainty for âT and for c is quoted.  Propagation, however, is superficial: time and mass uncertainties are ignored, the calculation method is not shown in detail, and quoted uncertainties for c appear inconsistent with the data.  Thus there is some consideration with significant omissions, fitting the middle of the 3-4 band (score 3).

Relevant methodological considerations (control of mass, heating time, room conditions, insulation, assumptions) are discussed and linked to data sufficiency.  However, the explanations are brief and do not fully explore why each choice secures ârelevant and sufficientâ data (e.g. no justification of three trials or power accuracy).  This aligns with the âdescribedâ level of the 3-4 band; the stronger end (4) is awarded because several factors are addressed.

Specific weaknesses such as energy losses, impurities, stirring inconsistency and reaction time are listed and their impacts are described qualitatively.  The relative magnitude of each limitation is not analysed in depth, so the work meets the âdescribes specific weaknessesâ level (3-4 band).  Because multiple pertinent points are covered with clear impact statements, a 4 is appropriate.

Step-by-step procedures, apparatus list, diagrams, control of variables, mass, time and power values, plus three repeats are all provided in sufficient detail for another student to reproduce the investigation.  Minor ambiguities (e.g. exact kettle model, calibration details) are present but do not prevent replication; therefore this comfortably meets the 5â6 descriptor, with a score of 5 recognising the small omissions.

Processing is attempted (energy equation rearranged, averages and graphs produced) but contains major conceptual and numerical errors: units are mixed (J vs kJ), calculated specific heat capacities are roughly double accepted values, and the inverse relationship is therefore based on flawed numbers.  These constitute âmajor inaccuraciesâ, placing the work in the 1-2 band; a 2 is awarded because calculations are at least completed consistently.

The student suggests realistic, relevant improvements (insulate kettle, use pure substances, automate timing, cover container).  For several of these, a rationale is provided that links the improvement to the identified weakness, but the explanations are brief rather than fully developed.  This fits the âdescribedâ descriptor of the 3-4 band; a 4 is assigned for covering many weaknesses with sensible remedies.

Raw and processed data are tabulated with units and uncertainties; graphs include axes and a fitted line.  Presentation is clear but occasional imprecision appears (mis-labelled averages, cramped table headings).  Both clarity and reasonable precision are mostly achieved, satisfying the 3-4 band; the higher mark (4) is given because most conventions are correctly followed.

The student briefly claims that results âaligned closely with literature valuesâ and mentions the First Law of Thermodynamics, but provides no numerical comparison or discussion of how far results deviate from accepted data.  This is a superficial comparison, matching the 1-2 band; a 2 is awarded as at least some external science is referenced.

The conclusion restates the observed inverse relationship and cites the high correlation from the graph, making it generally consistent with the (flawed) analysis.  It is not fully justified because the analysis itself is inaccurate and no quantitative comparison to accepted values is used to support the claim.  This fits âdescribed but not fully consistentâ, so band 3-4, lower mark 3.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated, but could be strengthened by specifying the application context or a concrete system (e.g., a particular industrial process).
The formula $$Q=mc\Delta T$$ is correctly introduced and positioned, making the theoretical basis clear and accessible.
The introduction effectively outlines the broad significance of specific heat capacity in sustainable development, but lacks a specific real-world scenario to anchor the research question in context.
The assumptions table is comprehensive, but explanations of why each assumption holds could be expanded to explain the rationale behind the controls.
Control variables are well chosen and justified, demonstrating reproducibility of the investigation.
Dependent variable description is clear, but the method for ensuring constant energy input across liquids could be elaborated (e.g., power calibration details).
The control variables table correctly identifies means of control, but the discussion of thermostat calibration is minimal. A calibration certificate or procedure should be referenced.
Apparatus list is detailed but lacks the exact model or calibration data for the kettle power rating. Include model number and calibration uncertainty.
The table listing molecular structures is informative, but the header rows are cramped and labels are unclear, hindering precise communication of data.
Selecting five liquids with distinct molecular interactions shows careful methodological planning and relevance to the research question.
Step-by-step setup is clear, but stirring is described qualitatively. Specify stirring rate or use a magnetic stirrer to reduce variability.
Raw data table presents temperature values clearly, but units for specific heat capacity are mislabeled (should be JÂ·kgâ»Â¹Â·Kâ»Â¹, not kJÂ·kgâ»Â¹Â·Kâ»Â¹).
Processed data shows calculated specific heat values that are roughly double expected literature values, indicating a systematic error in data processing.
Sample calculation mixes joules and kilojoules without conversion steps. Always maintain consistent units, e.g., convert 48 000 J to 48 kJ if reporting kJ/kgÂ·K.
The algebraic steps for $$c=\frac{Q}{m\Delta T}$$ lack clarity. Break down each substitution and include units in each step.
Calculated specific heats table is well organized and includes uncertainties, improving precision and clarity.
The graph displays an inverse trend clearly, but error bars are missing. Including uncertainty bars would demonstrate consideration of uncertainties.
Error propagation covers temperature measurement but omits uncertainty contributions from mass and power. Include propagation of all relevant variables.
Combining uncertainties as 0.1 + 0.1 = 0.2 K is correct but the reasoning step should show how each term contributes to final propagated uncertainty.
The analysis section concisely restates trends and connects to theory, demonstrating a coherent interpretation.
A percentage error of 11.5% is mentioned without showing its calculation. Include the computed and theoretical values and the formula used.
The conclusion cites a high correlation coefficient but does not address how data inaccuracies might affect model fit; qualify the strength of that correlation.
Comparison to literature values is stated but lacks numerical benchmarks; include actual literature values and discuss deviations.
The evaluation section lists methodological strengths clearly, demonstrating awareness of random error reduction.
The weaknesses table proposes improvements but does not discuss the relative impact of each limitation on results; rank or quantify impacts where possible.
Future extensions consider new liquids and methods realistically, showing thoughtful planning for further study.
Suggestions to insert a constant-power heater are well linked to identified heat-loss issues, but need explanation of expected accuracy gains.

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
rubricData.name: Physics IA
